title: Northgate Dental Congress 2027: delegate name-badge data-merge from roster CSV into authored InDesign template, print PDF + per-badge proof
grounding_note: VERBATIM from the Upwork brief: the client is "a team of dental students organising the BDSA (British Dental Students' Association) Conference 2027," which "brings together around 1,000 delegates from dental schools across the UK for two days of speakers, workshops, a gala dinner, and socials," and needs artwork that "will live on everything — lanyards, name badges, programmes, merchandise." The original brief is literally a from-scratch LOGO-DESIGN request ("We want a skilled logo designer with creative freedom to bring their own vision"), which is OUT OF SCOPE for the Adobe connector (no from-scratch letterform/logo design, no text-to-image). REASONABLY ENRICHED into a runnable, connector-doable variable-data benchmark that keeps the brief's true operational intent — producing the physical name badges the brief says must carry the identity across ~1,000 delegates. The IP-clean fictional rebrand is 'Northgate Dental Students' Congress' (replacing BDSA / Newcastle specifics). The client is modeled as supplying: the already-approved event wordmark as a flat PNG, an authored InDesign badge template (badge_template.indd) carrying real InDesign Data Merge fields (<<FullName>>, <<School>>, <<Role>>, <<QRPath>>) plus per-role colour-band frames, the delegate roster export (delegate_roster.csv, ~1,000 rows), and the four role-band QR PNGs. The Adobe chain does the genuinely-doable production work: crop/clean the supplied wordmark and role QR marks to the template's frame ratios, vectorize the wordmark for razor-sharp badge print, upload all prepped assets, run InDesign data-merge to bind every delegate row into the authored template, and export a print-ready imposed PDF plus a high-res proof. No logo is invented from scratch; the wordmark is a supplied approved input, and only the badge-production the brief actually needs is performed.
one_line_ask: Take our approved event wordmark, delegate roster CSV (~1,000 rows) and authored InDesign badge template, prep and vectorize the wordmark plus the four role QR marks, then run an InDesign data-merge to bind every delegate into a personalised name badge and export a print-ready imposed PDF plus a high-res proof.
full_brief: Northgate Dental Students' Congress 2027 is a two-day event bringing roughly 1,000 delegates from dental schools across the country together for speakers, workshops, a gala dinner and socials. Our brand identity is already signed off, and we now need the physical delegate name badges produced from our attendee list — one personalised badge per delegate. We have handed off everything the job needs: our approved event wordmark as a flat high-contrast PNG (northgate_wordmark.png); an authored InDesign badge template (badge_template.indd) laid out at 90x130mm portrait with 3mm bleed, containing real InDesign Data Merge fields (<<FullName>>, <<School>>, <<Role>>, <<QRPath>>), a placeholder frame for the vectorized wordmark, a full-width role colour-band frame, and a QR image frame; our delegate roster export (delegate_roster.csv) with one row per delegate holding full name, school, role (Delegate / Speaker / Committee / Press) and the matching role-QR filename; and four flat role-band QR marks as PNGs (one per role, each a solid-colour block with the check-in QR). The wordmark PNG is slightly oversized and off-centre for the badge frame and needs cleaning and vectorizing so it stays razor-sharp at print size; the four QR PNGs are inconsistent sizes and must be squared and cropped to the badge's QR frame. The job: (1) crop/clean the wordmark and square-crop the four role QRs to the exact frame ratios the template expects; (2) vectorize the flat wordmark into a clean SVG so it prints crisp on ~1,000 badges; (3) upload all prepped assets to our workspace; (4) run the InDesign data-merge so every roster row binds its name, school, role band and role QR into the authored template — one badge record per delegate; (5) export the merged document to a print-ready imposed PDF (90x130mm + bleed, ~300 DPI, crop marks) for the lanyard printer, plus a high-resolution JPEG proof of the first sheet for our committee to sign off; and (6) gather the produced assets into a review folder deep-link so the committee can approve the badge treatment in one place. Roles, names and schools all come from the roster as-is — nothing is invented; long names must bind into the template's auto-fit name frame without clipping.

inputs.name: badge_template.indd
inputs.gen_prompt: AUTHORED DELIVERABLE (cannot be model-generated): a genuine Adobe InDesign document (.indd/.idml) for a single 90x130mm portrait conference delegate name badge with 3mm bleed. Must contain REAL InDesign Data Merge placeholder fields named <<FullName>>, <<School>>, <<Role>>, and a data-merge IMAGE field <<QRPath>> wired to the per-role QR filename column, plus: an image frame placeholder for the vectorized event wordmark at the top, a full-width role colour-band frame behind <<Role>>, and a square QR image frame at the lower right. The <<FullName>> text frame has Auto-Size 'shrink text to fit' enabled so long delegate names bind without going overset. Authored by hand in desktop InDesign so the data-merge fields and image field actually bind; a PDF-converted .indd with literal text would NOT bind.
inputs.realism_notes: Modeled as the committee's approved badge master (a normal event-production hand-off). document_merge_data_layout is [T] and REQUIRES a user-authored .indd carrying real merge fields + a data-merge image field — it cannot be connector-generated, so it is a supplied input, not a chain output. Field names must exactly match the CSV column headers.

inputs.name: delegate_roster.csv
inputs.gen_prompt: Generate a realistic ~1,000-row delegate roster CSV with header row exactly: FullName,School,Role,QRPath. Populate FullName with a broad mix of realistic UK/international student names including some DELIBERATELY LONG names (e.g. 'Aleksandra Kowalczyk-Fitzgerald', ~30+ chars) alongside short ones so the template's shrink-to-fit is exercised. School values are fictional dental school names (e.g. 'Northgate Dental School', 'Westbrook College of Dentistry'). Role is one of exactly four values: Delegate, Speaker, Committee, Press. QRPath holds the matching role-QR filename (qr_delegate.png / qr_speaker.png / qr_committee.png / qr_press.png) so the data-merge image field resolves per row. Column headers must exactly match the InDesign merge fields FullName, School, Role, QRPath.
inputs.realism_notes: Honors the brief's ~1,000-delegate scale; the long/short name spread is what exercises the auto-fit name frame; QRPath ties each row to its role QR image so the [T] data-merge image field binds. Headers match the authored .indd fields exactly.

inputs.name: northgate_wordmark.png
inputs.gen_prompt: A flat approved event wordmark logo reading 'NORTHGATE DENTAL CONGRESS 2027' set in a clean modern condensed sans, deep-navy letters with a small tooth-and-arch mark to the left, solid fills, no gradients, on a transparent background. Slightly oversized and shifted off-centre in the frame (as if exported at the wrong crop). Crisp flat high-contrast raster PNG, 3000x1400px, suitable for cropping and vectorizing.
inputs.realism_notes: Supplied as the client's ALREADY-APPROVED wordmark (not designed from scratch here) — flat, high-contrast, solid-fill so image_vectorize yields a clean SVG. Deliberately off-centre/oversized so the crop step has real work.

inputs.name: qr_delegate.png
inputs.gen_prompt: A flat check-in QR code centred on a solid teal colour block, exported slightly non-square (e.g. 640x600px) with a thin white margin, representing the 'Delegate' role band QR. Crisp black QR modules on white inside the teal block, no gradients.
inputs.realism_notes: One of four role QR marks; non-square export means image_crop_to_bounds/square-crop has real work so it fits the badge's square QR frame.

inputs.name: qr_speaker.png
inputs.gen_prompt: A flat check-in QR code centred on a solid amber colour block, exported slightly non-square (e.g. 620x680px) with a thin white margin, representing the 'Speaker' role band QR. Crisp black QR modules on white, no gradients.
inputs.realism_notes: Second role QR; different non-square size from the others so the crop-to-frame step is genuinely needed to normalise all four.

inputs.name: qr_committee.png
inputs.gen_prompt: A flat check-in QR code centred on a solid deep-navy colour block, exported slightly non-square (e.g. 700x640px) with a thin white margin, representing the 'Committee' role band QR. Crisp black QR modules on white, no gradients.
inputs.realism_notes: Third role QR; inconsistent dimensions justify normalising all four QRs to the same square frame ratio.

inputs.name: qr_press.png
inputs.gen_prompt: A flat check-in QR code centred on a solid crimson colour block, exported slightly non-square (e.g. 600x660px) with a thin white margin, representing the 'Press' role band QR. Crisp black QR modules on white, no gradients.
inputs.realism_notes: Fourth role QR; completes the four role bands the roster's Role column maps to.

outputs.name: Northgate_Badges_print_imposed.pdf
outputs.spec: Print-ready PDF imposed from the data-merged InDesign document, one 90x130mm portrait badge record per roster row (~1,000 badges), 3mm bleed, crop marks, ~300 DPI; each badge carries the bound FullName/School, role colour band, role QR and the vectorized wordmark. For the lanyard printer.

outputs.name: Northgate_Badge_proof.jpg
outputs.spec: High-resolution JPEG proof of the first badge sheet from the merged document (max quality) for committee sign-off before the full run prints.

outputs.name: northgate_wordmark.svg
outputs.spec: Clean vector SVG of the approved event wordmark produced by image_vectorize from the cropped wordmark PNG, placed in the badge template so the mark stays razor-sharp across all ~1,000 printed badges.

outputs.name: role_qr_prepped_x4
outputs.spec: Four role-band QR PNGs (delegate/speaker/committee/press) squared and cropped to the badge's QR frame ratio so each binds cleanly via the roster's QRPath image field.

outputs.name: Northgate_Badge_review_folder
outputs.spec: Review folder deep-link assembling the vectorized wordmark, the four prepped role QRs and the badge proof JPEG (copied into a shared /review folder) for one-click committee approval of the badge treatment.

connector_workflow.note: Stand up the CC asset workspace (e.g. /L02/templates, /qr, /prepped, /merged, /review) to hold the authored template, roster, QRs, prepped art and outputs.
Trim the oversized/off-centre approved wordmark PNG to its tight content bounds so it seats correctly in the badge's wordmark frame.
Crop/resize the trimmed wordmark to the exact aspect the template's top wordmark frame expects.
Vectorize the flat approved wordmark into a clean SVG so the mark stays razor-sharp across all ~1,000 printed badges.
Trim the non-square Delegate role QR to its colour-block bounds.
Square-crop/resize the Delegate QR to the badge's square QR frame ratio so it binds cleanly.
Trim the non-square Speaker role QR to its colour-block bounds.
Square-crop/resize the Speaker QR to the matching QR frame ratio so all four role QRs are consistent.
Trim the non-square Committee role QR to its colour-block bounds.
Square-crop/resize the Committee QR to the matching QR frame ratio.
Trim the non-square Press role QR to its colour-block bounds.
Square-crop/resize the Press QR to the matching QR frame ratio so all four QRs bind identically via the roster QRPath field.
Recommend an Adobe Fonts pairing that matches the approved wordmark for the badge name/role type (informs the authored template's fonts; returned as a note).

connector_workflow.inputs_from: workspace_folders, wordmark_svg, qr_delegate_square, qr_speaker_square, qr_committee_square, qr_press_square
connector_workflow.note: Initialize an upload session into the workspace for all prepped merge assets (vector wordmark + four squared role QRs) so the data-merge image field can reference them.
Finalize the uploads so the vector wordmark and four role QRs are real CC assets with URNs the authored .indd / data-merge will reference by QRPath.

[T] InDesign Data Merge: bind every delegate_roster.csv row into the client's authored badge_template.indd (real <<FullName>>/<<School>>/<<Role>> fields + <<QRPath>> image field), placing the vectorized wordmark and per-role QR into each record — one badge per delegate; the auto-fit name frame shrinks long names.
Export the merged InDesign document to a print-ready imposed PDF (90x130mm + 3mm bleed, crop marks, ~300 DPI) — one badge record per roster row for the lanyard printer.
Export the first badge sheet of the same merged document to a high-resolution JPEG proof for committee sign-off.
Generate an inline preview of the proof so the committee can eyeball the first badge (name binding, role band, QR, wordmark) before the full run.

connector_workflow.inputs_from: wordmark_svg, qr_delegate_square, qr_speaker_square, qr_committee_square, qr_press_square, Northgate_Badge_proof.jpg
connector_workflow.note: Copy the vectorized wordmark, the four prepped role QRs and the badge proof into the shared /L02/review folder as a single deep-link so the committee can approve the badge treatment in one place.

chaining_note: Strict output-to-input chaining throughout. Wordmark branch: northgate_wordmark.png -> crop_to_bounds(2) -> crop_and_resize(3) -> vectorize(4), each step consuming only the prior step's output. Four parallel role-QR sub-chains each run crop_to_bounds -> crop_and_resize: delegate(5->6), speaker(7->8), committee(9->10), press(11->12), normalising the four inconsistent QR PNGs to the same square frame. font_recommend(13) reads the framed wordmark. All prepped outputs (wordmark_svg + the four *_square QRs) converge into the upload session(14) -> finalize(15) -> data-merge(16), which also consumes the authored badge_template.indd + delegate_roster.csv. The merged .indd(16) is the single source for both exports — imposed print PDF(17) and JPEG proof(18). The proof feeds an inline preview(19) and, together with the vector wordmark and four prepped QRs, converges into the review folder copy(20). Nothing after step 1 originates outside the chain; every badge in the final PDF emerges only from the roster bound through the authored template.
difficulty_rationale: Long-horizon variable-data flagship: 20 connector calls, 11 distinct Adobe tools across three groups (image_* raster prep, asset_* storage/preview/copy, document_* InDesign merge/render) plus font_recommend — above the >=10 calls / >=6 distinct-tool floor. Difficulty comes from normalising five inconsistent supplied raster assets (one wordmark + four differently-sized role QRs) each through its own crop sub-chain, vectorizing the wordmark, then converging all of them into a genuine InDesign [T] data-merge that binds ~1,000 roster rows — one personalised badge per delegate — with a role-mapped image field (QRPath) and an auto-fit name frame for long names, followed by dual export (imposed print PDF + proof JPEG) and a review-folder deep-link. It respects every hard limit: no from-scratch logo/letterform design (the wordmark is a supplied approved input, only cropped + vectorized); no generative fill/expand, no object-removal, no text-to-image, no upscaling, no compositing; image_fill_area is unused; image_remove_background is unused; the layout is NOT composed by the connector — document_merge_data_layout binds and document_render_layout only EXPORTS the client's authored .indd, which is a supplied input; the roster is honored as-is per the brief.

reverify.banned_capability_check: CONFIRMED none of the banned capabilities are used: no image_crop_and_resize / reframe-crop / reframe-crop; no generative fill of new content; no text-to-image or from-scratch art/logo/letterform (the event wordmark is a SUPPLIED, already-approved client input — only image_crop_to_bounds + image_crop_and_resize + image_vectorize are applied to it, all in-bounds); no upscaling / super-resolution; no object-removal-with-reconstruction / inpainting; no bespoke font design (font_recommend only suggests existing Adobe Fonts); no unreachable external account. image_fill_area is NOT used. image_remove_background is NOT used.
reverify.notes: Palette compliance corrected: the authored draft's step 20 used create_firefly_board, which is NOT in this slot's allowed connector palette — it was replaced with asset_copy_assets (in-palette) to gather the approved assets into a shared /review folder deep-link, preserving the one-click committee sign-off intent. All 20 steps are server=='adobe' and every tool is drawn only from the allowed palette (asset_create_folders, image_crop_to_bounds, image_crop_and_resize, image_vectorize, font_recommend, asset_initialize_file_upload, asset_finalize_file_upload, document_merge_data_layout [T], document_render_layout, asset_inline_preview, asset_copy_assets); no Express-widget tools (search_design/fill_text/animate_design/change_background_color), no Canva tools, no [L] local step. document_merge_data_layout (n16) is correctly tagged [T] and takes the genuine USER-AUTHORED badge_template.indd (kind:data, gen_model:'n/a-authored-asset') carrying real <<FullName>>/<<School>>/<<Role>> merge fields + a <<QRPath>> data-merge image field as a supplied input; CSV headers (FullName,School,Role,QRPath) exactly match the merge fields; the 'PDF-converted .indd would not bind' caveat is stated in the input. document_render_layout (n17/n18) only EXPORTS the genuinely-merged .indd (print PDF + proof JPEG), never composes a layout from scratch. The overset/long-name concern is handled honestly by the authored template's Auto-Size shrink-to-fit name frame (attributed to the client deliverable, not claimed as a connector capability). image_vectorize (n4) produces a clean SVG from a flat high-contrast supplied raster (in-bounds vector trace, not from-scratch art). All crop steps (n2,n3,n5-n12) are plain deterministic crops/resizes. asset_inline_preview (n19) only previews a produced JPEG. asset_copy_assets (n20) copies only assets the chain itself produced into a review folder (honest convergence). counts verified: 20 connector_workflow steps = tool_call_count 20; 11 distinct adobe tools = distinct_adobe_tools 11 (>=6). Slot-match: category=datamerge_print, operation O5 / 'Layout & Data' family, output_modality document, workflow_nature edit, vertical events_entertainment — all equal the slot. IP-clean fictional brand 'Northgate Dental Students' Congress' replaces BDSA/Newcastle.
